scenario: A team has a piece of tax-calculation logic applyTax(amount, region) they want to reuse from three separate top-level decisions in the same DRD: Pricing, Refund, Discount. They consider authoring it as one BKM and referencing it from all three. Does Camunda 8 support this cross-decision BKM sharing?
question: Can a single BKM be invoked from multiple decisions in Camunda 8?

options.text: 3 specification pattern but exceeds Camunda 8's current BKM scope restriction. 8. 3 spec's cross-decision BKM sharing via Knowledge Requirement graph edges works in Camunda 7's full DMN engine but has not been carried over to Zeebe's DMN engine.

Yes, Camunda 8 automatically generates a copy of the BKM for each decision that requires it -- no such automatic BKM duplication mechanism exists in Camunda 8's DMN implementation. There is no auto-copy feature; teams wishing to share logic across decisions must explicitly create separate BKMs per decision or use the recommended workaround of extracting the shared logic into a separate top-level Decision with Literal Expression body plus informationRequirement edges.

Each of the three decisions (Pricing, Refund, Discount) must maintain its own separate copy of the tax calculation BKM -- while this approach technically works, it violates the DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself) principle and creates maintenance risk: updating tax logic requires changing three separate BKM copies. The recommended workaround is extracting the shared logic into a separate top-level Decision Literal Expression and connecting the three consumer decisions to it via informationRequirement edges.

md:64 "Currently, you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision" (Pattern #34). 3 spec allows cross-decision BKM sharing via Knowledge Requirement edges in the DRD, but Camunda 8's DMN engine has not implemented that yet. Canonical C8 workaround: extract the shared logic into a separate Decision with a Literal Expression body (FEEL function-style body); each consumer Decision declares an informationRequirement edge to that shared Decision; the engine evaluates the shared Decision once per evaluation and the result is available to every dependent Decision.

a.text: Incorrect. Per dmn.md:64 verbatim: "Currently, you can only reuse BKMs within the same decision." This is a known C8 limitation (Pattern #34). The DMN-1.3 spec models BKMs as graph-level reusable functions referenced from any Decision via Knowledge Requirement edges; Camunda 8's DMN engine has not implemented cross-decision BKM invocation in 8.8 -- autosuggestion + invocation are scoped to the BKM's enclosing Decision only. Canonical C8 workaround: promote the shared logic to a separate top-level Decision with a Literal Expression body (acts as a reusable computed value). Per dmn.md:23-28 Modeler supports "Decision (tables and literal expressions)" -- a Decision Literal Expression is the closest functional equivalent to the DMN-spec BKM. Each consumer Decision then declares an informationRequirement edge to that shared Decision in the DRD; the engine evaluates the shared Decision once per evaluation and propagates the result. Trade-offs: Cross-decision sharing: Decision-with-Literal-Expression pattern ✅; BKM ✗. Parameterised invocation: shared Decision evaluates against its own inputs (decision-graph inputs); BKM accepts arguments at the call site. For "call with arbitrary args" semantics, you have to encode args as Input Data + propagate via DRD edges. Within-decision reuse: BKM works fine -- define once, call multiple times from a single Decision's Literal Expression body via FEEL function-call syntax. DRD shape (workaround): ` [Input: amount, region] → [Decision: ApplyTax (Literal Expression body)] ↓ informationRequirement [Decision: Pricing], [Decision: Refund], [Decision: Discount] each declare → [ApplyTax] ` Watch for: exam-style questions assuming DMN-1.3 spec cross-decision BKM sharing -- that pattern works in Camunda 7 with the Camunda DMN engine but not in C8's Zeebe DMN engine.

b.text: Incorrect. DMN-1.3 spec pattern not implemented; dmn.md:64 explicitly constrains BKM reuse to same-decision.

c.text: Incorrect. No auto-copy mechanism; explicit modeling required.

d.text: Correct. Functions but violates single-source-of-truth; option (a)'s shared-Decision pattern is the recommended approach.

explanation: Functions but violates single-source-of-truth; option (a)'s shared-Decision pattern is the recommended approach.
